channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: New Google commercial imagines a Declaration of Independence written with help from AI
item.description: Two hundred and fifty years after the signing of the Declaration of Independence, a new commercial asks: What if the Founding Fathers had access to Google Workspace?

item.title: Clean Edges: Using a PNG Alpha Mask on AI-Generated Animations
item.description: I'm building something new for Isovalent Labs. I can't say much yet, but there's an announcement coming. What I can tell you about is a technical rabbit hole I fell into along the way:...

item.title: How I Built a Product Hunt Scraper That Tracks Launches in Real-Time
item.description: How I Built a Product Hunt Scraper That Tracks Launches in Real-Time Every day, hundreds of new products launch on Product Hunt. Keeping track of them manually? That's a full-time job nobody...

item.title: 🧩 How to Pass a Request Body in a GET Request? Meet the New HTTP QUERY Method (RFC 10008)
item.description: ⚡ A New HTTP Verb for Developers The QUERY method (RFC 10008, June 2026) solves a long‑standing pain point: sending complex request bodies safely and cacheably without abusing GET or POST. Because...

item.title: Adding Homemade TLS to a Homemade Web Server
item.description: Original series: Building Your Own Web Server + Rebuilding TLS from Scratch This article connects two earlier series. In the web-server series, I rebuilt the HTTP side from raw sockets up to...

item.title: backon — The Modern Python Retry Library You've Been Waiting For
item.description: backon — The Modern Python Retry Library You've Been Waiting For If you've written Python code that talks to external APIs, databases, or network services, you've needed retry logic. The Python...

item.title: We deployed a LangChain agent for a client and it silently failed for two weeks. Here's what we built to make sure it never happens again.
item.description: Six weeks ago, a LangChain agent we'd deployed for a B2B client started failing on roughly 30% of its sessions. No exceptions. No 500s. Nothing in the logs that looked wrong. The agent kept...

item.title: Catching AI Red-Handed in Financial Data
item.description: When I was building security auditing tools like Git Secret Scanner, the rules were binary: a vulnerability exists, or it doesn't. But when you start building Generative AI pipelines for...

item.title: Why AI agents need three types of memory (and how I built all of them)
item.description: Most "agent memory" today is one thing wearing three hats: a vector database.You embed the past, you retrieve the nearest neighbor, you paste it into the prompt. It works until it doesn't, and...

item.title: MarketNow 2.0: 5 languages, 63 countries, 675 npm downloads — 2 weeks in
item.description: Two weeks ago we launched MarketNow — the trust layer for agent commerce. Here's what happened. By the numbers Metric Value Requests (Cloudflare) 55,807 Countries 63 npm weekly downloads 675...

item.title: Triage tenant maintenance requests with a property-management agent
item.description: Most "AI for property management" pitches start with a model that reads a leasing manager's inbox and "helps them keep up." That's fine until you realize the bottleneck isn't reading — it's that a...

item.title: NASA launched an emergency mission to stop the Swift Observatory from crashing to Earth
item.description: The Swift Observatory was launched in 2004, but recent solar storms have pushed its orbit lower, and it's in danger of burning up in Earth's atmosphere as soon as this year. To try and stave off...

item.title: Midjourney wants Hollywood studios to reveal the details of their AI usage
item.description: As part of an ongoing legal dispute with three Hollywood studios, Midjourney is seeking to compel those studios to reveal how they use AI themselves.

item.title: Alibaba reportedly bans employees from using Claude Code
item.description: Alibaba has reportedly classified Claude Code as high-risk software.

item.title: White House deletes thousands of web pages about energy conservation as heatwave slams US
item.description: The US Department of Energy reportedly deleted about 6,000 pages related to energy conservation as a historic heatwave tears across the country. The deletion was suspiciously timed, following...

item.title: Matic’s robot vacuum is getting a $250 price hike in September
item.description: The Matic is our favorite robot vacuum by a pretty comfortable margin. If you’ve been thinking about buying one, you may want to plan on doing it sooner than later. The company will raise its...

item.title: What is Mistral AI? Everything to know about the OpenAI competitor
item.description: Mistral AI, which offers some open source AI models, has raised significant funding since its creation in 2023, with the ambition to “put frontier AI in the hands of everyone.”

item.title: Flatbush Zombies’ Erick the Architect misses his BlackBerry keyboard
item.description: Erick the Architect is a founding member of, and the primary producer for, the legendary Flatbush Zombies. He's toured the world, performed on Kimmel and Fallon, played Coachella, and collaborated...

item.title: Hey number pad lovers, this is a keyboard we can finally agree on
item.description: I know a vocal group of people who swear by the number pad on their keyboard. And yet, for years I haven't cared about using one - until I put my hands on the Epomaker RT98. It's a mechanical...

item.title: The square-ish phone that I wanted to love
item.description: The Ikko MindOne Pro is delightfully small. I keep calling it a square phone, which isn't quite right; the screen is square, but the phone itself is slightly rectangular. The camera flips up so...

item.title: U.S. Government Entity Paid Kairos $1 Million in Data-Theft Extortion Case
item.description: A U.S. government entity paid about $1 million to keep stolen files from being leaked, according to a new case study by Rakesh Krishnan for Ransom-ISAC, built on a leaked negotiation chat and the...

item.title: The fanfiction community is at war with AI — and itself
item.description: Over the past week, a new fanworks movement has kicked off, with the aim to root out authors using generative AI. But the detection methods being implemented are questionable, and any fanfic...

item.title: North Korean Hackers Publish 108 Malicious Packages and Extensions in PolinRider Campaign
item.description: The North Korean threat actors linked to the Contagious Interview campaign have been observed publishing 108 unique packages and web browser extensions spanning npm, Packagist, Go, and Google...

item.title: Qi fan fan
item.description: Despite my initial skepticism, I'm now sold on wireless Qi chargers that add integrated fans to keep your phone cool while charging. I figured they'd be too loud, or too weak, or too gimmicky, but...

item.title: The only AI glossary you’ll need this year
item.description: The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. Here is a glossary with definitions of some of the most important words and phrases you might encounter.

item.title: Unpatched Flaws Disclosed in Filesystem Bundled Into Millions of Embedded Devices
item.description: Security firm runZero has disclosed seven vulnerabilities in FatFs, a small filesystem library that lets a device read and write the FAT and exFAT formats used on USB drives and SD cards. The...

item.title: New "Bad Epoll" Linux Kernel Flaw Lets Unprivileged Users Gain Root, Hits Android
item.description: A newly disclosed Linux kernel flaw called Bad Epoll (CVE-2026-46242) lets an ordinary user with no special access take full control of a machine as root. It affects Linux desktops, servers, and...

item.title: New Avalon Malware Framework Packs CrownX Ransomware Capabilities
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously undocumented modular malware framework codenamed Avalon that's distributed by means of a multi-stage phishing chain capable of bypassing...

item.title: The browser wars aren’t about search anymore — here are the best alternatives to Chrome and Safari
item.description: We’ve compiled an overview of some of the top alternative browsers available today aiming to challenge Chrome and Safari.

item.title: A device that revives eyeballs from dead donors could make eye transplants possible
item.description: It’s not easy to transplant a whole human eye. The surgery is difficult. And the eyes themselves start to degenerate as soon as they’ve left the body. When surgeons attempted it a few years ago,...
